metric_name: Weekly PC market trend commentary
metric_value: EMachines has released a new line-up of desktops in time for the opening of the "back-to-school" shopping season. As we've come to expect from eMachines, these PCs offer lots of features for not a lot of cash.
notes: Kastner market assessment for week of 2003-07-06

metric_name: BestBuy: buying tips for week of 2003-07-06
metric_value: Best Buy has revamped its website completely. You'll find the link to its "weekly ad" at the bottom, center of the home page along with the "store locator" link.

metric_name: Circuit City: buying tips for week of 2003-07-06
metric_value: Save up to $250 on select Desktop and Laptop Computer Packages and enjoy no interest for 12 months. Circuit City's weekly ad had only two PCs, but these were two more new models from eMachines.

metric_name: CompUSA: buying tips for week of 2003-07-06
metric_value: CompUSA.com tends to continue selling "older" PC models after BestBuy.com and CircuitCity.com have removed them. This means you can get better deals on models that retailers want off the shelves to make room for the newer versions.

metric_name: Dell: buying tips for week of 2003-07-06
metric_value: Dell's deals include a free memory upgrade on all the 2350, 8300, and XPS models. With the exception of the 2350, all models offer free CD upgrades in the second bay.

metric_name: eMachines: buying tips for week of 2003-07-06
metric_value: Circuit City and Best Buy sell most of eMachines' new line-up and they continue to sell a few older models. Bundled deals from these national retailers supply the best bang for your buck on eMachines desktops, except in the case of the T2615 that is only appears at www.emachin...

metric_name: Gateway: buying tips for week of 2003-07-06
metric_value: Gateway's prices on the PCs we customize at www.gateway.com have not changed, with the exception of a slight price drop on the 500X model this week. But we were happy to see that the 700XL, while maintaining the same price, now comes standard with the new Pentium 4 HT 3.2 GHz ...

metric_name: Sony: buying tips for week of 2003-07-06
metric_value: Sony.com offers a plethora of models right now, although, like eMachines, the best deals usually come from bundled retail offers. Receive free shipping at Sony Online this week.

metric_name: HP and Compaq: buying tips for week of 2003-07-06
metric_value: HP Online offers competitive prices on its retail models, although national retailers usually carry well-priced bundled deals. HP has expanded its A250 line to resemble the S4000 series of desktops by Compaq so that choosing the grade of processor determines the model you will...

metric_name: Intel Pentium 4 HT: positioning in week of 2003-07-06
metric_value: Customize a high-end PC with the latest Hyper-Threading Intel processor with 3.2 GHz continues to yield high prices, especially in contrast with HP's winning machine in our $1600+ category so we have not included any Dell PCs with this technology yet.

metric_name: Intel Pentium 4: positioning in week of 2003-07-06
metric_value: Customize a high-end PC with the latest Hyper-Threading Intel processor with 3.2 GHz continues to yield high prices, especially in contrast with HP's winning machine in our $1600+ category so we have not included any Dell PCs with this technology yet.

metric_name: AMD Athlon: positioning in week of 2003-07-06
metric_value: The T2615 offers a 2600+ AMD Athlon processor and dual optical drives, as well as 512 DDR and 120 GB hard drive for only $649 (unbundled).
